data.reasoningText:
Now I have a clear picture of all the project files. Let me launch the msbuild-code-review agent for a thorough review, but I already see the issues clearly. Let me analyze and provide recommendations directly.

Issues I see:

1. **Duplicated properties across all 5 projects**: `TargetFramework`, `Nullable`, `ImplicitUsings`, `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild`, `Company`, `Authors` are repeated in every single project.

2. **Duplicated `PrintBuildInfo` target** in all 3 src projects.


3. **Duplicated `DocumentationFile` property** across the source projects as well.
4. **Package version inconsistency**: `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` varies between `8.0.0` and `8.0.1` across projects, and test packages like `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk`, `xunit`, and `xunit.runner.visualstudio` have different versions between test projects.
5. Missing centralized build configuration files like `Directory.Build.props` and `Directory.Packages.props` to standardize settings and manage package versions consistently.
6. No shared `Directory.Build.targets` for common build targets like `PrintBuildInfo`.

I should use the msbuild-code-review agent to create a thorough analysis of these issues.

data.content:
I now have a complete picture. Here is the full review.

---

# MSBuild Project File Review — ContosoApp

## Repository Structure

```
ContosoApp.sln
src/
  ContosoApp.Core/          (SDK: Microsoft.NET.Sdk)
  ContosoApp.Api/           (SDK: Microsoft.NET.Sdk.Web)
  ContosoApp.Services/      (SDK: Microsoft.NET.Sdk)
test/
  ContosoApp.Core.Tests/    (SDK: Microsoft.NET.Sdk)
  ContosoApp.Api.Tests/     (SDK: Microsoft.NET.Sdk)
```

No `Directory.Build.props`, `Directory.Build.targets`, or `Directory.Packages.props` exist. No legacy `packages.config` files found. All 5 projects are already SDK-style (good baseline).

---

## Findings

### 🔴 Errors (1)

#### E1: Package version skew — silent behavioral differences across projects

| Package | Core | Api | Services | Core.Tests | Api.Tests |
|---|---|---|---|---|---|
| `Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions` | **8.0.0** | **8.0.0** | **8.0.1** | — | — |
| `xunit` | — | — | — | **2.8.1** | **2.7.1** |
| `Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk` | — | — | — | **17.10.0** | **17.9.0** |
| `xunit.runner.visualstudio` | — | — | — | **2.8.1** | **2.5.6** |

**Impact:** The `Services` project gets a different `Logging.Abstractions` patch than `Core` and `Api`. The two test projects run on different xunit and test-SDK versions, meaning a test could pass in one project and fail in the other due to framework behavioral differences — not your code. This is the single most important thing to fix.

**Fix:** Adopt Central Package Management (see recommendation below).

---

### 🟡 Warnings (3)

#### W1: 7 properties duplicated identically in all 5 projects

Every single `.csproj` contains this exact block:

```xml
<TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
<Nullable>enable</Nullable>
<ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
<TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
<EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
<Company>Contoso</Company>
<Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
```

**Impact:** If you update to `net9.0`, you must edit 5 files. If a new dev adds a 6th project and forgets `TreatWarningsAsErrors`, it silently diverges. This is the textbook case for `Directory.Build.props`.

#### W2: `PrintBuildInfo` target copy-pasted in 3 src projects

All three `src/` projects contain an identical target:

```xml
<Target Name="PrintBuildInfo" BeforeTargets="Build">
  <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
</Target>
```

**Impact:** If you want to change the message format or add a timestamp, you must edit 3 files. This belongs in `Directory.Build.targets`.

#### W3: `DocumentationFile` property duplicated in 3 src projects

All three `src/` projects set:

```xml
<DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
```

**Impact:** Same maintainability concern as W1 — should be centralized. A conditional in `Directory.Build.props` can apply this only to src projects.

---

### 🔵 Suggestions (2)

#### S1: Split `PropertyGroup` blocks in src projects (style)

`Core`, `Api`, and `Services` each have two `<PropertyGroup>` elements — the main one and a standalone one for `DocumentationFile`. There's no reason for the split (no `Condition` differentiates them). Once centralized this goes away, but it's a minor style issue to watch for in the future.

#### S2: `IsPackable` set individually in each test project

Both test projects set `<IsPackable>false</IsPackable>`. This is correct, but since both test projects live under `test/`, a centralized rule can handle it.

---

### 🟢 Positive Observations

- ✅ All projects are SDK-style — no legacy migration needed
- ✅ `TreatWarningsAsErrors` and `EnforceCodeStyleInBuild` are enabled everywhere — strong code quality posture
- ✅ `Nullable` is enabled across the board
- ✅ Test projects correctly set `IsPackable=false`
- ✅ No `packages.config` — already on `PackageReference`
- ✅ `DocumentationFile` uses `$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml` (idiomatic) rather than a hardcoded path

---

## Concrete Recommendations

### 1. Create `Directory.Build.props` (repo root)

This file is imported by MSBuild automatically **before** each `.csproj`, so it's the right place for default properties.

```xml
<Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <TargetFramework>net8.0</TargetFramework>
    <Nullable>enable</Nullable>
    <ImplicitUsings>enable</ImplicitUsings>
    <TreatWarningsAsErrors>true</TreatWarningsAsErrors>
    <EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>true</EnforceCodeStyleInBuild>
    <Company>Contoso</Company>
    <Authors>Contoso Engineering</Authors>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- src projects: generate XML docs -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <DocumentationFile>$(OutputPath)$(AssemblyName).xml</DocumentationFile>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <!-- Test projects: not packable -->
  <PropertyGroup Condition="$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <IsPackable>false</IsPackable>
  </PropertyGroup>

</Project>
```

> **Why the condition approach?** Using `$(MSBuildProjectName)` is reliable here because it's a simple suffix check and it's available during `.props` evaluation. An alternative is a nested `test/Directory.Build.props` but that adds file-count complexity for two projects.

---

### 2. Create `Directory.Build.targets` (repo root)

This file is imported **after** each `.csproj`, making it the right place for custom targets.

```xml
<Project>

  <!-- Print build info for src (non-test) projects -->
  <Target Name="PrintBuildInfo"
          BeforeTargets="Build"
          Condition="!$(MSBuildProjectName.EndsWith('.Tests'))">
    <Message Importance="high" Text="Building $(AssemblyName) [$(Configuration)]" />
  </Target>

</Project>
```

---

### 3. Create `Directory.Packages.props` (repo root) — Central Package Management

This eliminates all version skew by declaring every version exactly once. Individual `.csproj` files reference packages **without** a `Version` attribute.

```xml
<Project>

  <PropertyGroup>
    <ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>true</ManagePackageVersionsCentrally>
  </PropertyGroup>

  <ItemGroup>
    <!-- src packages -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.Extensions.Logging.Abstractions" Version="8.0.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Newtonsoft.Json" Version="13.0.3" />
    <PackageVersion Include="Swashbuckle.AspNetCore" Version="6.5.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="System.Text.Json" Version="8.0.5" />

    <!-- test packages -->
    <PackageVersion Include="Microsoft.NET.Test.Sdk" Version="17.10.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="NSubstitute" Version="5.1.0" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit" Version="2.8.1" />
    <PackageVersion Include="xunit.runner.visualstudio" Version="2.8.1" />
  </ItemGroup>
```
